summary: coin batch now supports --csv (flatten the JSONL stream into a single CSV with a header row) paired with --columns (comma-separated dotted paths, e.g. cert_no,data.Name,_keep.box). Missing fields emit empty cells, not the literal string "null". When --columns is omitted under --csv, the header is auto-discovered from the first row's cert_no + top-level _keep.* + data.* keys. New helper file coin_batch_csv.go isolates dotted-path lookup and value→cell shaping.
reason: AGENTS.md P7 — a one-shot pipeline from cert list to enriched flat CSV currently needs 30+ lines of Python post-processing on the batch JSONL. With the unified flat shape from PR #683, the dotted-path projector is small (<200 LOC) and reuses the existing global --csv flag. Pairs naturally with the AGENTS.md 2026-05-18 inventory workflow (parse Google Sheet → batch with _keep round-trip → CSV upload).
validated_outcome: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...; live smoke: `coin batch --file pcgs-coin-list.csv --csv --columns "_keep.box,_keep.slot,cert_no,data.Name,data.Year,data.Grade,data.PriceGuideValue"` writes a valid CSV with one header row + one row per cert; David Hall null PriceGuideValue renders as an empty cell; `Images` column is absent from auto-headers (confirms P6 strip is also live)
